briefdescription.para: Set supported reset action.

parameterdescription.para: indicates if reset status check is supported
indicates if pulse reset action is supported
indicates if reset assert/deassert is supported

simplesect.para: Here bit fields are used decide supported actions as defined below: BIT 1 - Assert/Deassert BIT 2 - Pulse BIT 3 - Reset status Bit set indicates corresponding action is supported and vice versa.

briefdescription.para: Check if assert/dessert reset is supported.

parameterdescription.para: is supported reset actions

briefdescription.para: Check if pulse reset is supported.

parameterdescription.para: is supported reset actions

briefdescription.para: Check if Status check is supported.

parameterdescription.para: is supported reset actions

briefdescription.para: Read the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to read

briefdescription.para: Write the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to write
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

briefdescription.para: No delay, no power state check.
Delay, no power state check.
Delay, power state check.
Invalid pulse type.
This typedef defines type of pulse reset to be executed for peripherals.

detaileddescription.para:
3 type of pulse reset are possible:
Pulse with no delay and no power state validation
Pulse with delay but no power state validation
Pulse with delay and power state validation

briefdescription.para: This typedef defines reset actions on the peripherals.
This typedef defines resetIDs of peripherals maps to PMUFW resetIDs.

detaileddescription.para: This resetIDs are not offsetted by 1000 and are relative.

briefdescription.para: This typedef defines possible values for reset status of peripherals.
Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XResetPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xresetps.h for the definition of XResetPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a specific reset controller instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called before other functions of the driver are called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XResetPs instance.
is the config structure.

briefdescription.para: Assert reset for specific peripheral based on reset ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XResetPs instance.
is the ID of the peripheral.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if reset assertion was successful.

briefdescription.para: Deassert reset for specific peripheral based on reset ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XResetPs instance.
is the ID of the peripheral.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if reset deassertion was successful.

briefdescription.para: Pulse reset for specific peripheral based on reset ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XResetPs instance.
is the ID of the peripheral.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if pulse reset was successful.

briefdescription.para: Get reset status for specific peripheral based on reset ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XResetPs instance.
is the ID of the peripheral.
is the status of reset for ResetID. 1 if asserted and 0 if released
